strengths: 15+ years of hands-on software engineering across web, mobile, embedded, and microservices at scale
Founder of an open-source geo-data platform demonstrating end-to-end product ownership and Kubernetes deployment
Recent exposure to AI-agent infrastructure and MCP tooling at current role
concerns: No demonstrated AI/ML expertise, published AI products, or platforms — only ~1 year of peripheral agent-tooling work versus a role demanding 'recognized expertise in AI'
Career trajectory is Senior/Staff individual contributor; no evidence of Director-level influence, strategic advisory work, or inspiring large internal/external audiences
Minimal cloud-platform depth (some AWS CDK at Optilyz, Kubernetes for a personal project) — far short of the cloud-strategy fluency expected of a Principal advising strategic customers

key_factors.factor: Lack of recognized AI expertise or shipped AI products
Shallow cloud-platform and architecture leadership footprint

response.justification: The candidate is a competent senior/staff full-stack engineer with broad language exposure and a long-running open-source side project, but the resume shows no track record matching a Director-level Principal Engineer in AI Strategy: no advanced AI degree, no recognized AI products or platforms, no evidence of advising strategic customers, and no demonstrated influence at the scale or altitude this role requires. Recent AI-adjacent work is limited to roughly a year of agent-tooling contributions, and cloud experience is tactical rather than strategic, making this a weak fit for the position.
